title: Lessons from the Lab — Retrospectives
body: Post-engagement breakdowns from real lab boxes. Eight chains so far (Exghost, Vault, Shenzi, Sona, Hacktrack, Payday, Snookums, plus the AD Auth capstones) covering file-upload RCE, AD compromise, XAMPP/WordPress, custom-protocol brute force, the AD enumeration loop, and two contrasting routes to a DC (roast-to-memory-theft vs spray-to-kerberoast). Each one is a complete chain — recon to root — with timing, the specific decisions that mattered, and the reusable patterns extracted from them. Read these between machines, not during one. The methodology is the asset; the specific CVEs rotate.
warn: These are after-action reports, not technique references. If you're stuck on a live box, use the Jump menu or mindset_stuck. Come back here once the machine is rooted.

choices.label: Vault — AD chain (SMB → NTLMv2 → WriteDacl → DCSync)
Sona — Custom TCP brute + Java EL injection + PwnKit
Hacktrack — AD enum loop + ForceChangePassword chain
Payday — LFI → SSH brute → passwordless sudo
Tool output lies — five debugging lessons
AD Auth Capstones — two chains to the DC
